title: The Library of Dreams
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A dream researcher discovers a hidden wing of sleep where every person's dreams are stored as books — and someone is rewriting them.
long_description:
Dr. Mina Park has not dreamed in seven years. When a patient reports a recurring dream of a door that does not exist in waking life, she uses an experimental lucid-dreaming device to follow him across the threshold — and finds herself in the Library of Dreams, an infinite archive where every dream ever dreamed is preserved as a physical book.

Guided by the enigmatic Librarian, Park discovers that someone has been systematically rewriting the dreams of trauma survivors, removing pain and replacing it with hollow comfort. The Editor, a neurologist who entered the Library twenty years ago and never left, believes she is healing humanity. But her rewrites are causing the Library to crumble and the Dreamstream — the river of raw sleep that feeds all human consciousness — to poison itself.

To save the Library and the dreamers it serves, Park must confront the Editor, restore the corrupted texts, and face her own buried grief. A story about memory, loss, and the truths we can only find in sleep, The Library of Dreams asks whether a life without pain is a life worth living.
main_characters: Dr. Mina Park — A sleep researcher who has not dreamed in seven years
The Librarian — An ageless being who curates the Library of Dreams
The Editor / Dr. Elena Vasquez — A neurologist lost in the dream realm for twenty years
Daniel Mun — The patient whose recurring dream of a door opens the way to the Library
Marcus Vasquez — Elena's late husband, a composer
